name: Latent Space (newsletter + podcast + Discord, swyx & Alessio Fanelli)
audience_size_estimate: ~170,000-190,000 newsletter subscribers (Latent Space investor report, newsletterinsights.io, 2026)
fit_reason: AI-engineer audience overlaps directly with 'solo builder + AI research system' positioning; technical depth matches TradingMapClaw's architecture-forward content
risk: Finance-adjacent content needs careful framing to avoid implying investment-advice endorsement; large publication may have high sponsorship minimums

name: Ben's Bites (AI newsletter, Ben Tossell)
audience_size_estimate: ~120,000-150,000 subscribers (Ben's Bites About page and AI for Automation profile, 2026)
fit_reason: Builder/founder audience curious about AI-assisted engineering workflows, matches the 'zero coding background, AI as engineering partner' narrative
risk: High-volume daily/twice-weekly AI news format may not have room for finance-adjacent research content; needs clear non-advice framing

name: TLDR AI (newsletter, TLDR network)
audience_size_estimate: ~1.1 million readers (TLDR AI homepage / Readless review, June 2026)
fit_reason: Massive reach among engineers and technical professionals; could be a channel for the dual-engine architecture story as a build-in-public feature
risk: Extremely broad audience, low % overlap with market-research-specific readers; sponsorship slots are likely competitive/expensive

name: The Batch (DeepLearning.AI newsletter, Andrew Ng)
audience_size_estimate: Large technical ML/AI readership (cited among top 4 technical AI newsletters per Leland AI newsletter guide, 2026)
fit_reason: Rigorous technical synthesis audience; well-suited to a verification/cross-check architecture story rather than a hype piece
risk: Very high editorial bar; unlikely to accept sponsored content, better suited to earned coverage/pitch than paid partnership

name: r/algotrading (Reddit community)
audience_size_estimate: ~150,000+ members (PainOnSocial subreddit list for quant analysts, 2025; historical growth documented on Reddit itself)
fit_reason: Direct audience match: quantitative trading, statistical methods, programming — exactly the technical crowd TradingMapClaw's dual-engine architecture post targets
risk: Strict no-self-promotion / Sunday-only promo rules in some periods; must be builder-transparent, not sponsored-post friendly (community, not a paid-placement channel)

name: r/LocalLLaMA (Reddit community)
audience_size_estimate: 500,000+ members with an official Discord launched at that milestone (MegaOne AI, 2026)
fit_reason: Open-source / local-model AI builder audience; matches the multi-model council + local Qwen3 14B fallback design story
risk: Community strongly favors open-source/self-hosted framing; a commercial research product needs careful, transparent, non-promotional framing

name: Flirting with Models (podcast, Corey Hoffstein / Newfound Research)
audience_size_estimate: Established systematic-investing podcast covering value/momentum/managed futures strategies (Apple Podcasts, Bloomberg company press, 2026)
fit_reason: Audience of systematic/quant investment professionals and enthusiasts; strong fit for the maker-checker / cross-verification research-method narrative
risk: Guest-interview format on a professional-grade show; sponsorship or guest slot would need a strict non-advice, research-only framing to avoid misalignment with the show's institutional audience

name: Papers With Backtest (podcast + YouTube + course)
audience_size_estimate: Estimated 100-1,000 monthly listeners on podcast per FeedSpot algo-trading podcast directory (2026), plus an active YouTube channel and course business
fit_reason: Directly aligned niche: research papers → backtested strategies → practical application, mirrors TradingMapClaw's research-method content (S-Factor, cross-verification, supply-chain mapping)
risk: Smaller audience size; also sells a paid course, so any partnership needs to be clearly delineated from cross-promotion of competing paid products

name: The Plain Bagel (YouTube, Richard Coffin CFA/CFP)
fit_reason: Rigorous, non-hype personal finance/investing education channel; explicitly research-and-education framing matches TradingMapClaw's compliance posture (CFA/CFP-credentialed host who is known for calling out 'finfluencer' hype)
risk: Very large channel — likely selective about sponsorships and may require paid-placement budgets beyond a research-tool startup's early-stage reach

name: Two Cents (PBS Digital Studios personal finance YouTube)
audience_size_estimate: Long-running PBS Digital Studios finance-education series (100K+ subscriber milestone documented on channel; ongoing PBS-affiliated production)
fit_reason: Beginner-friendly financial literacy framing; good fit for the 'research and education, not advice' compliance message to a general audience, without technical AI jargon required
risk: PBS affiliation likely means formal editorial/sponsorship review process; broad general-audience fit is weaker than technical-audience candidates

name: Indie Hackers (community, run by Courtland Allen / Stripe)
audience_size_estimate: Long-established solo-founder/bootstrapper community with an active build-in-public culture (referenced across build-in-public guides, 2026)
fit_reason: Direct audience match for the 'zero coding background, one Mac mini, $7/month' solo-builder narrative; strong precedent for builders sharing technical + personal founder stories
risk: Community is skeptical of overt self-promotion without genuine engagement; needs authentic participation, not a one-off pitch

name: Build in Public (X/Twitter community, 262K+ members)
fit_reason: Large, active solo-founder/build-in-public audience on the same platform where the X launch content already lives; natural distribution for the System Build Log narrative
risk: Twitter Community engagement quality varies; needs consistent authentic posting rather than a single cross-post to be effective

name: Disability Thinking Weekday (newsletter, Andrew Pulrang)
audience_size_estimate: Weekday newsletter linking to disability issues/culture articles (cited in Uxcel accessibility newsletter roundup, 2026)
fit_reason: Direct audience match for the 'Not pity. Visibility.' story angle — disability-and-tech builder narrative without any market/finance framing required
risk: Niche/smaller reach; content fit is strong for the personal story but not for the market-research product itself — must stay strictly story-focused, no product pitch

name: AbilityNet Newsletter (assistive technology, UK charity)
audience_size_estimate: Established accessibility/assistive-tech newsletter for people with disabilities and older adults (Uxcel accessibility newsletter roundup, 2026)
fit_reason: Good fit for the 'AI-assisted engineering let me build at a pace my hand couldn't type at alone' story — technology-enables-accessibility angle
risk: UK-charity context; audience is technology-accessibility focused rather than finance/markets, so any collaboration should stay story/accessibility-angle only, not product-sales angle

name: Codú (community for AI builders & indie hackers)
audience_size_estimate: Active AI-builder/indie-hacker community with a weekly 5-minute newsletter (Codú About page, 2026)
fit_reason: Directly targets AI builders and indie hackers — matches both the technical build story and the solo-operator business-model story (r/SaaS-style content)
risk: Newer/smaller community relative to Indie Hackers or Latent Space; lower immediate reach but potentially higher engagement quality
